entry.title: Data Transfer between SAP Successfactors ALM to Incentive Management using APIs (TrueProducer APIs)
entry.content:
What is SAP SuccessFactors Agent Lifecycle Management?

With real-time insights into agent effectiveness, hierarchies and relationships, training, and compliance information, including certifications, you can better engage with your Insurance agents and empower them to support your company’s mission and goals. 

Data Transfer between SAP Successfactors ALM to Incentive Management via API Orchestration 

Introducing Incentive Management APIs to consume data from ALM.

Note : 

  • Below APIs are under feature flag option, Kindly contact SAP Support Team for enabling the services in your tenants.
  • Kindly check in table : CS_InstalledProduct contains your subscription for ALM tenant provisioned and integrated with Incentive Management.
  • Below APIs are still undocumented in https://api.sap.com and purpose of the blog is to provide Partners, customers to make use of the below APIs till such time

To try copy the below template and paste in Visual Studio Code with RestClient Extension Installed

@clientid = XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX
@clientsecret = XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX
@k8 = https://XXXX.app.commissions.cloud.sap
@ias = https://XXXXXXXX.accounts.ondemand.com
@password = YYYYYYYYYYYYYYYYYYY       
@username = XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX



######################  Portal Users ######################################
###  API Documentation
### https://XXXX.app.commissions.cloud.sap/usersvc/apidoc/#

###
#  login
POST {{ias}}/oauth2/token?grant_type=client_credentials
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Authorization: Basic {{clientid}}:{{clientsecret}}

###
@accessToken = {{login.response.body.access_token}}


###
GET {{k8}}/mtsvc/tcmp/rest/v2/contractTypes
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/mtsvc/tcmp/rest/v2/appointmentTypes
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/mtsvc/tcmp/rest/v2/contractTypes
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/mtsvc/tcmp/rest/v2/producerTypes
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/mtsvc/tcmp/rest/v2/producerAddressTypes
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/mtsvc/tcmp/rest/v2/licenseTypes
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/mtsvc/tcmp/rest/v2/statusCodes
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}


###
GET {{k8}}/mtsvc/tcmp/rest/v2/licenseLines
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/mtsvc/tcmp/rest/v2/licenses
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/mtsvc/tcmp/rest/v2/appointments
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/mtsvc/tcmp/rest/v2/jurisdictions
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/mtsvc/tcmp/rest/v2/appointmentLines
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

###
GET {{k8}}/mtsvc/tcmp/rest/v2/lineOfAuthorities
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

Examples of all GET Method

entry.title: Re: Hide/show totals of a SAC Story Table dimension dynam...
entry.content:
Hello Emil,

Unfortunately, the "Totals" properties of a table and/or a dimension in the table is not a part of the script API.

Optimized Story Experience API Reference Guide Version 2025.1

So no - there really is no way to achieve what you want with script.

Note: The same is true for the "Analytical Application".

entry.title: RITA activation for GB and CZ Country Codes in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

We use SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

We want to activate RITA  - Registration of Indirect Taxation Abroad for first time. Our current scenario is as follows:

1. Tax Code " ZC = Czech 21% output service"  is defined as EU Tax Code in Country GB, Tax Procedure 0TXGB.

2. Invoice will be created in Country GB using above Tax Code ZC (which is a Czech tax code define in GB tax procedure 0TXGB).

3. This tax code ZC will be reported in Country CZ (Czech Republic)  CZ VAT Declaration.

4. Scope Item 5RP is not active in our System.

5. Country GB assigned to Company Code 5110 and Country CZ assigned to Company Code 5430.

So could you please help me how to activate RITA? whether it should be activated to Company Code 5110 with countries GB and CZ or to Company Code 5430 with countries GB and CZ?

 

Thank you.

Regards,

Shrinivas

entry.title: Re: API to POST to SAP | RAP | SEGW | if_http_extension~h...
entry.content:
Hello Sathya,

If you are going to use in your task RAP Object ( Root+Child etc..) as suggested, you can use unmanaged save. see docu: Integrating Unmanaged Save in Managed Business Objects 

Here you can use your BAPI in the class and its method 

save_modified

here you have all you need. Your JSON data are in the importing parameters, because you have defined your rap object root+ child and you can read data of it within  query.

Regards

Jarda

entry.title: SAP Databricks is now GA. Get the most of it skilling yourself with Mosaic AI
entry.content:
Words are cheap. I will be crisp.

SAP is a goldmine of data. The classic old Data Engineering challenge has been unifying that complex, scattered business data – especially from SAP applications like ECC and S/4HANA – with other enterprise data, and then truly leveraging it for advanced analytics, and for some reasons (like MRP or Forecasting), Machine Learning.

The new challenge is to do the same, but for cutting-edge generative AI. To tackle this, SAP introduced early this year the SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC).

BDC has 3 main concepts and 3 main SAP products. The 3 main components are the Data Products, the Insight Apps and the BDC itself. And the products that make it possible are the classics SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud (which have been around for a while) and SAP Databricks, which is an optional component of BDC and was in preview... until today.

Now that its GA; better get understand what it is, and what it's not.

What SAP Databricks IS: The union between SAP Business Data with Generative AI

Do not confuse Databricks with SAP Databricks. Very similar, but different. What SAP Databricks is;

  • A Native, Fully Managed Service within SAP Business Data Cloud: This isn't an external solution bolted on. SAP Databricks is a version of the Databricks platform included natively as a service within SAP BDC. It is a fully managed service, meaning SAP handles the underlying infrastructure, allowing us to focus on our data and AI initiatives.
  • Unifying Semantically Rich Business Data with Leading AI/ML: The core power lies in unifying our valuable, semantically rich data from SAP applications with the current industry-leading AI, machine learning, data science, and data engineering platform. SAP achieves the goal to integrate SAP data with the rest of the business data for the reasons why Databricks is booming. Its advanced analytics and AI use cases.
  • Zero-Copy Data Sharing Powered by Delta Sharing: SAP moves away from data replication. BDC is an end destination and SAP Databricks leverages zero-copy Delta Sharing. This enables seamless, bidirectional data sharing between SAP BDC and Databricks, so data does not need to leave the SAP platform to use it for Generative AI use cases. This is crucial for integrating data products from SAP applications with semi-structured and unstructured data from any source, without needing to move the data physically.
  • Custom Data Products: Within SAP Databricks, users can easily access SAP data products and create custom data products out of their AI/ML and analytics workloads. Data products are fundamental building blocks within BDC which I explained in this blog post, serving as packaged and governed data assets.

What Databricks tools are available in SAP Databricks.

  • Databricks Notebooks: Ideal for pro-code data engineering tasks and building custom AI/ML models.
  • Databricks SQL: Allows us to analyze datasets at scale using standard SQL queries.
  • Unity Catalog: Provides centralized governance for all our data assets, including structured and unstructured data, ML models, notebooks, and files. It enables governance for data products exposed from SAP Databricks via the SAP BDC catalog.
  • Mosaic AI: This is our gateway to building secure, governed, and custom AI/ML solutions, including advanced Generative AI applications and Large Language Models. I'll discuss this below.
  • Serverless Spark: Provides scalable compute resources for our data processing tasks.

SAP Databricks and Databricks Feature Parity

The following table summarizes the availability status of key Databricks components in Standard Databricks versus SAP Databricks, based on the knowledge I could gather. Subject to be corrected.

 

Feature/Component Category

Feature/Component Name

Standard Databricks

SAP Databricks

Core Platform

Workspace UI

Yes

Yes (Through BDC)

 

REST APIs

Yes

Likely Yes (Potentially subset or different endpoints/authentication)

 

CLI

Yes

Unconfirmed

Compute

All-Purpose Clusters (Classic)

Yes

Likely Excluded (Focus on Serverless)

Yes

Likely Excluded (Focus on Serverless)

Yes

Likely Excluded

Yes

Yes

 

Serverless Notebooks/Jobs Compute

Yes

Yes

Yes

Yes

Yes

Yes

Yes

Yes

Yes

Yes

Yes (blog)

 

Jobs / Workflows

Yes

Yes (Likely Serverless execution)

ML/AI (Mosaic AI)

MLflow (Managed)

Yes

Yes

 

Model Registry (via MLflow)

Yes

Yes

 

Model Serving (Mosaic AI)

Yes

Yes

Yes

Yes

 

Vector Search (Mosaic AI)

Yes

Yes

Yes (Including forecasting for SAP)

Yes

Yes (With caveats regarding BDC connection)

 

The Generative AI with Mosaic AI

To me, the most innovative piece of what Databricks has to offer is Mosaic AI. I tell you why. Mosaic AI allows us to:

  • Build Modern Generative AI Solutions: Create cutting-edge GenAI applications directly on the Databricks platform, integrated with our business data.
  • Leverage the Full ML Lifecycle: Build an end-to-end GenAI solution using Databricks' infrastructure, data, and tools, covering everything from data preparation and management (like building knowledge bases for RAG) to model training, deployment, securing, and monitoring. This allows for greater control and potentially lower costs compared to relying solely on external, black-box GenAI solutions [implied]. Databricks' AI Agent Framework, powered by Mosaic AI, even allows building domain-specific AI agents that can call external APIs.
  • Ground LLMs with our Business Data: Combine the power of LLMs with any organization's specific, governed business data to create accurate and relevant AI applications. This is a key pattern, often using Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) techniques, which Mosaic AI supports.

I know this is going to bring questions I am yet not ready to answer.

How to get official knowledge on Databricks and Mosaic AI

SAP will be really detailed as usual to share how the Compute, the Data Catalog and the Delta Share works, because SAP Databricks brings a different UI compared to Databricks, it limits the Data Integration to external sources or destinations, and does not allow to deploy the infrastructure on prem or at the hyperscscaler of choice, but the rest is the same, and getting the best of Mosaic AI is on us.

If you have not used it, once you're comfortable with the basics, its key to understand how Databricks approaches Machine Learning lifecycle and how this is transfered to Generative AI;

Remember, Mosaic was acquired in 2023 and might look different since its specific for Generative AI.

  • Key Concepts:

    • MLflow: Understand how Databricks integrates this open-source platform for managing the ML lifecycle (tracking experiments, packaging code, registering and deploying models).
    • Feature Store: How to create, manage, and serve features for model training.
    • Model Training: Using libraries like scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch within Databricks notebooks.
    • Model Registry: Storing and versioning trained models (often within Unity Catalog).
    • Basic Model Serving: Understanding the concept of deploying models as APIs (aka Mosaic AI Model Serving).

There are too many concepts so lets summarize a Cheat Sheet

Mosaic AI Cheat Sheet

Feature/Concept

Description

Key Details / How it Relates

Mosaic AI (Databricks)

Databricks' brand for its generative AI offerings and a complete suite for AI and ML

Integrated technology from the MosaicML acquisition.  Provides a well-structured AI framework ensuring governance, inference, orchestration, and data management . Enables creation of a complete Gen AI solution using its own infrastructure, data, and tools  All-in-one platform for building, managing, and scaling GenAI applications . Offers complete ownership over models and data

Mosaic AI Agent Framework

A set of tools on Databricks designed to help developers build, deploy, and evaluate production-quality AI agents

Supports building various agent types like RAG applications, Text-to-SQL agents, data analyst agents, customer support, research, business operations, advisory agents, and more  Part of the Orchestration Layer in the Mosaic AI architecture  Integrated with MLflow and compatible with LangChain/LangGraph and LlamaIndex  Includes robust agent evaluation  Key functions include intent recognition, dialogue management, context tracking, knowledge augmentation, fact-checking, memory management, tools & planning . Includes Agent SDK, Agent evaluation, Agent serving, and a Tools Catalog . Leverages Unity Catalog, Model Serving, and AI Playground  Designed for rapid experimentation and deployment with control over data sources . Enables tool-calling and interaction with organizational data (Delta Tables) and external APIs  Supports human-in-the-loop via Review App  Includes Tracing for debugging

Mosaic AI Vector Search

An integrated vector database and search capability within Databricks

Went General Availability (GA) . Stores vector embeddings for fast similarity searches  Crucial component for building RAG applications  Index is built based on Delta tables  Can use Databricks foundation models for embeddings  Index is automatically synchronized with the source Delta tables  Supports similarity search via the Vector Search Endpoint  Part of the Storage & Knowledge Base Layer  Scalable to handle billions of embeddings and thousands of queries per second . Allows building a "Vector Database" internally without external options  Requires preparing a Delta table knowledge base  Supports incremental auto sync verification . 

Mosaic AI Model Training

Tools for fine-tuning or pre-training large language models

Integrated from the MosaicML acquisition  Part of the model lifecycle . Leverages scalable compute resources for demanding algorithms  Allows training custom LLMs at x lower cost . Requires selecting a base model (e.g., Llama , DBRX, etc.), specifying the type of task (code completion, instruction fine-tuning, continued pre-training), and providing the location of the dataset . Used by Shutterstock for a text-to-image model . Part of Mosaic AI capabilities . 

Mosaic AI Batch Inference

Enables running batch LLM inference at high scale

Uses the ai_query function to apply AI directly on data  Leverages Databricks Model Serving for speed, autoscaling, batching, and fault tolerance [Implicit in , using endpoints supported by ]. Used for tasks like summarization, sentiment analysis, and topic analysis on transcripts . Can use models like OpenAI Whisper V or open source models deployed on Databricks . 

Mosaic AI Gateway

Provides a unified interface and governance layer for querying and managing access to various AI model APIs (internal and external)

Part of the Governance Layer in the Mosaic AI architecture . Ensures compliance, security, and monitoring of AI requests . Includes features for Permissions & Rate Limiting , Payload Logging (for auditing) , Usage Tracking (for performance/engagement) , AI Guardrails & Traffic Routing , and Content Filtration & PII Detection . Builds on Model Serving . Unified interface to query foundation model APIs and audit usage  Supports any model endpoint (Azure OpenAI, Amazon Bedrock, Meta Llama , etc.) . Logs usage in Unity Catalog with permissions enforced . Provides real-time spending insights for cost management . Safeguards data and GenAI deployments with centralized governance . Ensures responsible AI deployment . Content safety and quality checks (related to guardrails) are also part of monitoring . 

Mosaic AI Model Serving

Component responsible for running AI models and serving responses in real-time or batch mode

Part of the Inference Layer in the Mosaic AI architecture . Provides a Unified Deployment Interface . Offers Scalable Model Deployment and Automatic Resource Scaling  Supports Model/Agent Endpoints  Agents registered in Unity Catalog can be deployed as Model Serving endpoints  Integrated with the Agent Framework . Enables real-time similarity search against the Vector Search index . Can serve custom LLMs . Used for Batch Inference via ai_query [Implicit in , ]. Part of Mosaic AI capabilities . Enables seamless model deployment  Supports real-time serving & batch inference  

Mosaic AI Agent Evaluation

Tools and processes within the Mosaic AI Agent Framework for assessing the quality, reliability, and safety of AI agents

Focuses on robust evaluation through human feedback loops, cost/latency trade-offs, and quality metrics  Includes LLM Judge and Agent Metric on parameters like chunk relevance, ground truth, query relevance, response safety, latency, token count . Agent performance is evaluated against a ground truth dataset  Uses the MLflow Evaluate API  Allows iterative evaluation by modifying datasets and rerunning . Enables logging of interactions (questions, answers, feedback) for performance analysis . Supports using LLMs or human judges (internal/external) for evaluation . Ensures quality, reliability, and safety  Human-in-the-loop via Review App is supported  Separate documentation is available . Part of the Agent Framework components . 

Mosaic AI Tool Catalog (in UC)

A registry within Databricks, managed in Unity Catalog, for registering functions (SQL, Python, remote) and model endpoints that AI agents can call

Part of the Mosaic AI Agent Framework . Supports Tools & Planning functionality . Helps clients create a registry of SQL, Python, or remote functions, and model endpoints . Agents can use tools from the catalog to interact with data sources (Delta Tables) or external systems/APIs  Uses Unity Catalog Functions (uc_functions) as tools  Enables tool calling . Managed and registered in Unity Catalog  Example includes using tools for financial analysis  

RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)

An architecture pattern for GenAI systems that uses retrieval of relevant data to augment model responses

Mosaic AI Vector Search is a crucial component for building RAG applications on Databricks  Involves indexing documents/data, converting to embeddings, and performing similarity search to retrieve relevant information for the LLM [Implicit in , ]. Helps reduce hallucinations  The Mosaic AI Agent Framework supports building RAG applications  Advanced RAG techniques exist beyond Naive RAG  Requires a foundation of good data governance and metadata  Uses vector databases  

AI Functions

Functions, such as SQL, Python, remote, or specific AI capabilities like ai_query, that can be registered and called by AI agents or used for AI tasks on data

Can be registered in the Tools Catalog . Can be Unity Catalog Functions (uc_functions)  Enables agents to interact with data or external systems  ai_query is a specific function for batch LLM inference  Used by agents to perform actions based on prompts  Example of using UCFunctionToolkit to get tools  

Unity Catalog

Databricks' unified governance layer for data, AI models, notebooks, files, and other assets

Provides centralized governance  Vector Search indexes are created and managed within Unity Catalog  The Tools Catalog for the AI Agent Framework is registered in Unity Catalog  AI Agents/Models are registered in Unity Catalog via MLflow  Enables model governance, ensuring only authorized users can access, modify, or deploy models . Logs AI Gateway usage with permissions enforced . Provides Attribute-based access control (ABAC) to all managed assets . Crucial for providing context and ensuring reliability for AI (agents, text-to-SQL) through metadata and governance  Facilitates seamless interoperability and data sharing (e.g., via Delta Sharing)  Manages metadata for Delta Lake tables, often the source for Vector Search  Supports lineage tracking [Implicit in , , ]. Key component for an AI governance program, converging data and AI metadata  Used in SAP Databricks for governed AI/ML  Essential for access control to prevent data/model mishandling . 

Delta Lake

An open standard table format providing reliability and performance for data lakes

Often the foundational storage format for data used in Databricks [Implicit in , , , , ]. Data tables used as a source for Mosaic AI Vector Search are typically Delta tables  Provides transaction logs, schema evolution, and time travel [Implicit]. Vector Search indexes are automatically synchronized with updates to source Delta tables  Used for storing historical data that agents can access . Enhanced Delta Lake is mentioned as a Databricks capability  Supports zero-copy data sharing (Delta Sharing)  

Foundation Models / External Models

Pre-trained large language models (LLMs) provided or accessible through the Databricks platform or other platforms, via APIs/endpoints.

Databricks provides access to foundational models (dbrx, llama, mixtral) . Can use third-party models like OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Amazon Bedrock, Meta Llama , Mistral, Google, Reka  Accessible via Model Serving Endpoints and potentially through the AI Gateway  Used for various tasks like generating embeddings for Vector Search or performing analysis in Batch Inference via ai_query . Agents can use specific LLM endpoints  Other platforms also integrate or offer foundation/external models (IBM watsonx, Snowflake Cortex)  

AI Agents

Applications designed to perform tasks by leveraging LLMs and external tools/functions

Built using the Mosaic AI Agent Framework  Can call tools (like Unity Catalog functions or external APIs) to retrieve information or perform actions  Can perform tasks like analyzing financial data (Investment Assistant) , answering questions based on internal knowledge bases (RAG) , or Text-to-SQL  Require data governance and metadata for accuracy and trustworthiness  Can be evaluated for quality and reliability  Can be deployed as Model Serving endpoints  Proliferation of agents creates new challenges like AI Agent silos and duplicate access control  Need a foundation of well-defined schemas, up-to-date metadata, and business glossary to thrive . Can potentially update metadata based on user feedback . 

Text-to-SQL

The capability for an AI system to translate natural language questions into SQL queries

A specific type of AI agent use case mentioned as supported by the Mosaic AI Agent Framework  Relies on understanding database schema and metadata to generate accurate SQL  Requires context, good data governance, and metadata to be successful  Agent accuracy is a concern, requiring trust in the application built around the AI . Mentioned as a tool an AI Agent can use . Can enable "precision analytics" by tailoring data transformations . 

AutoML

Automated Machine Learning.

Listed as an end-to-end AI/ML lifecycle capability available in the SAP Databricks context  Mentioned as Mosaic AI AutoML  Part of the collaborative and unified data science environment  

Managed MLflow

Databricks' integrated platform for managing the ML lifecycle

Integrated with the Mosaic AI Agent Framework  Supports experiment tracking, model logging  Enables model registration in Unity Catalog (UC)  Used for Agent Evaluation (MLflow Evaluate API)  Includes MLflow Tracing (open-source .) . Supports deployment workflows  Part of end-to-end AI/ML lifecycle capabilities  Managed MLFlow is listed as a capability  Supports iterative evaluation and tracking metrics . Captures code and environment for deployment . 

AI Playground

A sandbox environment on Databricks for prototyping and testing AI agents, particularly those using tool calling

Provides an environment for integrating tools (UC functions) with LLMs for testing  Allows refining agent functionality before deployment . Supports exporting the agent from the playground for further development or deploying it directly as a Model Serving endpoint  Can auto-generate basic agent package notebooks . Leverages AI Playground as part of the Agent Framework benefits . 

Feature Engineering & Serving (in UC)

The process of preparing data features for ML models, integrated within the Databricks platform, potentially leveraging an intelligent feature store

Feature Engineering is listed as a capability within the single Databricks stack  Mosaic AI includes an Intelligent Feature Store for automatic feature engineering and selection . Leverages data governed by Unity Catalog [Implicit]. Features are used by models typically deployed via Model Serving [Implicit]. (Note: "Feature Serving" is not explicitly named as a distinct component like Model Serving in the sources, although features are inherently served with the model that uses them.) Feature engineering is mentioned as part of the end-to-end AI/ML lifecycle  

Lakehouse Monitoring

A capability on Databricks for monitoring data, features, and AI models

Profiles, diagnoses, and enforces data quality . Automatically identifies trends and anomalies . Visualizes key metrics like data volumes, percent nulls, model drift, accuracy, F score, precision, and recall . Can inform users if models need retraining . Part of the five key stages of a GenAI solution . Integrated within Databricks platform  Includes diagnostics for data processing pipeline issues . Has built-in checks for data quality and content safety . Monitor data, features, and AI models in one place . Listed as an end-to-end AI/ML capability  Crucial for ensuring quality and appropriateness of AI-generated content . 

Inference Tables

Tables used to log interactions (questions, answers, feedback) from AI agents or models during inference

Primarily used for performance analysis and monitoring . Supports ensuring the quality, reliability, and safety of AI responses . Related to Agent Evaluation monitoring . 

AI Gateway Logging & Usage Tracking

Capabilities within Mosaic AI Gateway for recording user interactions and tracking system performance and user engagement

Part of the Governance Layer . Includes Payload Logging (records user interactions for auditing) . Includes Usage Tracking (monitors system performance and user engagement) . Enables auditing model usage and data sent/returned . Logs usage in Unity Catalog with permissions enforced . Provides real-time spending insights for cost management . Part of safeguarding data and GenAI deployments with centralized governance . 

Agent Evaluation Monitoring UI

User interface components, such as the Review App, supporting the monitoring, review, and analysis of AI agent performance and interactions

Supports reviewing agent query requests/responses . Allows stakeholders and human judges (internal/external) to give feedback and label responses  Used for iterative evaluation and tracking metrics . Enables analysis of logged interactions . The Review App is a specific mechanism for online evaluation and human-in-the-loop feedback  

Databricks AI/ML Documentation: Tutorials: Get started with AI and machine learning (AWS Example)

entry.title: Businees Use Case for SAP CAPM Node.js Application

entry.title: Re: Unexpected Crystal Reports Error: Invalid Printer Spe...
entry.content:
Hi Russell, see this blog I wrote around Label printers. 

See if using custom paper size names resolves the issue: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/printing-crystal-reports-custom-paper-sizes/ba-p/13396188

As I noted I don't see any CR errors in the dump file so it suggests these errors are coming from the Seagull/Zebra print drivers.

I worked a lot with Seagull Developers and they are very smart in dealing with their drivers. I suggest posting on their Forum or talk to their support team to see how to enable logging.

The errors are suggesting Windows is having issue communicating with the Printer.

NOTE: CR is simply passing the error to you from their dll's. Possibly suggesting a buffer overflow or something like that.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 2020 64 BIT Runtime only

entry.title: Is it possible to create Unique Project Name & Sequential Project ID in PS (Public Cloud)?
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I am currently implementing the Project System (CAPEX) process for a pharmaceutical client. During the User Acceptance Testing (UAT) phase, the client has raised two critical requirements that need to be addressed in order to proceed with the sign-off.

The points raised are as follows:

  1. Sequential Project ID Validation:
    The system should enforce sequential creation of Project IDs. For example, if Project ID ABC-01 exists, users should not be able to create ABC-03 unless ABC-02 has already been created.

  2. Unique Project Name Validation:
    The system should not allow the creation of multiple projects with the same name. Each project name should be unique across the system.

I kindly request your assistance in evaluating and providing a resolution or suitable workaround for these requirements at the earliest convenience, to avoid any delay in the UAT sign-off process.

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
Souhardhya Sen
PS Support

entry.title: COMING SOON! Introduction to Artificial Intelligence and SAP Business AI
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION:

AI has become important not only to businesses, but also to everyday users of technology. In this academic lesson, the value-add of ethical applications of AI is discussed in the context of SAP AI-enhanced solutions and business use examples.

The first activity of this Academic Lesson features a Learning Journey that explains the hierarchy of AI, machine learning, deep learning, and Gen AI; and showcases the progress from statistics alone to Gen AI. The confluence of technology, reliable enterprise data, and processes is highlighted as the differentiator for SAP Business AI versus other providers of AI.  

The second activity showcases an SAP Course that takes a theoretical approach to providing an explanation of machine learning and AI. It explains the concepts of how machines learn and provides some engaging practical examples and hands-on activities. This course also provides downloadable supplemental learning content in four (4) languages. Originally set up as a three-week course, it can be completed according to your class’s time frame and topic schedule.  

This Academic Lesson could be used as a precursor to the more technical Academic Lesson and Learning Journey on Large Language Models.  

SUGGESTED AREAS OF STUDY:

Any area of study that uses or addresses the topic of artificial intelligence.  It is appropriate for students at undergraduate and graduate level university, community college, trade school and upper-level secondary school.  

DOWNLOAD:

Microsoft Word (editable) format:  available May 7th

PDF (non-editable) format:  available May 7th

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports Viewer cannot print or export
entry.content:
CR for VS integrates into VS 2010->2022 so you need to say what CR for VS SP you are using?

You can get the latest SP from here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

AND YOU MUST USE THE SAME SP you Developed with on the Server where your app is running, don't mix versions.

And Check the PAM out to see what versions of Windows is supported with which version of CR for VS:

https://www.sap.com/documents/2016/06/f871031e-757c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa.html

And check this Blog O wrote for upgrading your project to the latest SP:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/upgrading-a-visual-studio-2008-gt-2022-net-project-with-the-latest-cr-net/ba-p/13469215

entry.title: Re: ASP.NET Report Viewer & multiple IIS Worker Processes
entry.content:
Hi Andy, Often R&D do not post fixed issues if someone hasn;t specifically request a fix, they may have found it while testing so often all of the things they do are not recorded puiblicly. All we can suggest is to use the latest SP and see if it resolves the issue because R&D don't fix older SP's and apply the fix in the next SP... One of the problems SAP DEV has is there are a lot of Third Party updates that constantly break CR/BOE products, it's impossibly to publicly document all of them, unless someone reports the issue. I agree with you, it would be nice to be able search for them all with results but SAP is complicated... lol

The workflow is the main BOE Product and CR/CR for VS are all built nightly and in the same build scripts. So something fixed in BOE may work it's way down into Crystal Reports and the runtime.

Unfortunately BOE/CRS fixes are gated, requires a support Contract, to be able to search for a relative reported issue/fix/solution. Only time CR and CR for VS fixes are made public is if it's only specific to those products and therefore the KBA is flaged as Public and available through searches etc.

Since I've retired I no longer have access to those Gated KBA's/SAP Notes so my searches are limited as well. I can sometimes ask the SAP Guys to do so or if I think they need to look into a reported issue I can ask them, since I no longer have access to the internal Builds and source code.

Anyways, thanks for reporting the issue, now others have the solution as well...

Thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: Provisioning between MRP storage areas
entry.content:
Hello

As long as the issuing storage location is set in the stock transfer order, the requirement will appear in the MRP Area when you are checking the MRP results in MD04, for example. 

MRP does not determine the issuing storage location, but you can use BAdI MD_EXT_SUP in Classic MRP for this purpose. I wrote this WIKI that explains how to use this BAdI:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/mrp/3138697892.html

If you are using MRP Live, you might consider method PURREQ_BEFORE_UPDATE_ADJUST of BAdI PPH_MRP_RUN_BADI. 

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Valuation area 002 cannot be the paying valuation area in acctg code 1000Message no. TPM_TRG155
entry.content:
I have faced following error while executing financial transaction through create financial transaction app in cloud public edition:

Valuation area 002 cannot be the paying valuation area in acctg code 1000

Message no. TPM_TRG155

Diagnosis

The valuation area 002 cannot be defined as the paying valuation area in
accounting code 1000. The accounting principle IFRS is assigned for
postings from this valuation area. However, the leading ledger is not
included in this accounting principle.

Procedure

Select a valuation area for payments that posts to the leading ledger.

entry.title: Opening UDO Form via Code
entry.content:
Opening UDO Form via Code

When attempting to open a User Defined Object (UDO) form using the following code:

  • Form? form = _app.OpenForm(BoFormObjectEnum.fo_UserDefinedObject, uid, null);
    uid is a FormType from an .srf file here, which was added to UDO dureing initialization.

The form does not open as expected.

In contrast, loading the form using XML with LoadBatchActions works:

  • _app.LoadBatchActions(xml);

However, this method prevents opening multiple instances of the same UDO.

Current Setup:

  • The UDO is registered with .srf file during the initialization of an object

  • .srf is properly associated through the UDO registration process

  • Despite this, OpenForm fails to open the UDO form.

What is the correct and complete sequence for programmatically adding and opening a UDO form in SAP B1?
And what is the correct approach in registering UDO in the system (with .srf file and etc).

entry.title: Introducing Application Frontend Service
entry.content:
As part of our exciting  SAP Build release, we're happy to announce the release of Application Frontend service, a new SaaS application on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) for hosting, serving and operating frontend applications.

Next Generation Solution for hosting frontend applications

Application Frontend serves as a single point of entry for hosting, serving and operating frontend applications. By consolidating all aspects of UI application management under one managed service, Application Frontend simplifies the development experience and enhances custom application operations.

Bridging the Gap Between Neo and Multi-Cloud

If you have been around SAP BTP for more than 10 years then, most likely, you have used the Neo environment. If you developed an HTML5 application in Neo, then you probably used the HTML5 Runtime. Many customers we met in the past years have reported that the snappy experience they had in Neo, when developing and operating HTML5 applications, was lost when moving to the multi-cloud environment, where the HTML5 Application Repository and the managed application router (approuter) are used.
This feedback was received, noted and motivated us to come up with a new solution for managing HTML5 applications. A solution that will bring back the simplicity and flexibility customers liked so much in the Neo environment. With Application Frontend, we aim to bring that experience into the multi-cloud environment.

The first capabilities Neo fans will find familiar are host-based routing, the versioning concept and a direct deployment option. Additional capabilities like extended control from the SAP BTP cockpit will be added in the future.

But don’t get me wrong, Application Frontend is not a replica of HTML5 Runtime in Neo. It is a multi-cloud service compatible with the multi-cloud development model. We use the same descriptors used in HTML5 Repository and application router, ensuring that developers don't need to learn yet another paradigm.

Very high level architecture diagram

 

A New SaaS application

Application Frontend is a SaaS application which is not coupled to any specific SAP BTP runtime environment or other SAP BTP applications. You can find and subscribe to the service from the SAP BTP cockpit. Application Frontend is also equipped with a service broker to support interaction flows using service instances, for example when using MTA deploy in the CF environment. 

While there is native support in the good old MTA concept for lifecycle management, Application Frontend also provides a command line interface (CLI) as a first-class tool for managing your applications. With the CLI, you can directly interact with the service and execute all actions supported by Application Frontend to enjoy fast response times and new integration opportunities.

 

Supported Use cases

At the moment, Application Frontend supports hosting of single tenant, stand-alone applications. (Stand-alone means applications that are not embedding other applications.)
In the future we will extend the supported use cases to cover more scenarios like applications dependencies, multi-tenancy, native SAP Build Work Zone integration, etc.
Application Frontend is perfect if you have a Build license and developing new HTML5 applications or migrating existing ones from Neo.
If the HTML5 Repository and application router are more suitable for your needs, please continue using them.

Application Frontend management UI in SAP BTP Cockpit

 Next steps

Application Frontend service is starting its journey. We will continuously release additional features to support new scenarios and delight your experience.
In few weeks’ time, we will also make Application Frontend available in the SAP trial environment, so you can try it out more easily.
Note: Application Frontend will reside next to the HTML5 Repository, and the managed Application Router (approuter) hosted in SAP Build Work Zone. Both services will continue to be available, supported and developed as needed.   

 How to get started

SAP Build is available for subscription, more details in SAP Discovery Center
Once you purchased SAP Build, follow these steps for Application Frontend on-boarding.

entry.title: Re: Auto language translation - SAP Webi
entry.content:
This may not be the best answer but it will get you part of the way there.

  1. Design the report in your first language.
  2. Copy the report table and paste it next to the first.
  3. Change the headers and any literal items to the second language.
  4. Set the table properties on the each of these tables, to be Hide When Formula Is True.
  5. Set each table's formula, to use the =GetContentLocale() function.  For instance, =GetContentLocale() <> 'en_US' will hide this table if the viewer profile's locale setting is not English US.  Set the other table to <> Arabic. 
  6. Align the tables so they are on top of each other.
  7. If a viewer's profile is set to English, the Arabic table will be hidden and they'll see the US version.  If the profile is Arabic, the English will be hidden and they'll see the Arabic version.
  8. Numbers and dates are automatically converted to format based on the Local setting preference.
  9. Each viewer will need to set their profile's location setting to their preferred language.

This will work for headings and literals but unfortunately, it won't translate data fields like names or product descriptions.  To do that, you may need to have alternate lookup tables for multiple languages.  I hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Field Values in Intelligent Event Processing custom e...
entry.content:
Hi, 2023_Kurt,
From built-in help in IEP, I see a definition for Event Name:


For Event Context, I do not see anything specific to IEP itself-> I will request this be added. 

It appears that the various Use Cases document what they want for Event Context :
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/filter-event-types?q=event+context

Business Process Monitoring:

  • Event Context: You can enter an asterisk (*) in this field or a pattern of the event name. For example if the event name is Billing Documents Not Posted to Accounting, you can enter the pattern, *Billing Documents*.

Business Service Management:

  • Event Context: Enter the service name as found in the Service Information tab of an event for this service. You can enter an asterisk (*) in this field or enter the pattern of the service name. For example if the service name is MyAIN_01, you can enter a pattern, *MyAIN*.


Health Monitoring:

  • Event Context: You can enter an asterisk (*) in this field.

Integration Monitoring:

  • Event Name: Enter the event name found under the Event Configuration section. You can enter an asterisk (*) in this field.

  • Event Context: You can enter an asterisk (*) in this field or a pattern of the event name. For example if the event name is Erroneous CPI messages, you can enter the pattern, *CPI*.


Job & Automation Monitoring

  • Event Context: You can enter an asterisk (*) in this field or a pattern of the event name. For example if the event name is Critical Execution Status for job detected, enter the patter *Critical Execution*.


    Hopefully this information is helpful to you. 
    Best regards,
    Paula

entry.title: SAP Developer News May 1st, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

PODCAST: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/05/01/sap-developer-news-may-1st-2025/

ABAP Challenge for May

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/sap-community-escape-house-for-may-abap-cloud/ba-p/14073134

UI5ers live

  • Webinar May 8th: https://www.linkedin.com/events/7321157596495699970/about/
  • Previous episodes: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLHUs_FUbq4dX8lUBM21QKGC_9CwCe6mBC

New adapters in SAP Integration Suite

  •  Anaplan: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/anaplan-adapter-for-sap-integration-suite/ba-p/14089229
  • HubSpot adapter: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/hubspot-adapter-for-sap-integration-suite/ba-p/14089261
  • Azure Cosmos DB: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/azure-cosmos-db-adapter-for-sap-integration-suite/ba-p/14089244
  • Amazon EventBridge: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/amazon-eventbridge-adapter-for-sap-integration-suite/ba-p/14089251
  • Google PubSub: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/google-pubsub-adapter-for-sap-integration-suite/ba-p/14089236
  • IBM MQ (REST) adapter: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/ibm-mq-rest-adapter-for-sap-integration-suite/ba-p/14089258
  • Salesforce PubSub adapter: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/salesforce-pub-sub-adapter-for-sap-integration-suite/ba-p/14089266
  • LeanIX adapter: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/leanix-adapter-for-sap-integration-suite/ba-p/14089267
  • RFC Adapter in Edge Integration Cell: https://help.sap.com/whats-new/cf0cb2cb149647329b5d02aa96303f56?locale=en-US&Component=Cloud+Integration;Edge+Integration+Cell

SAP Build Extensibility Wizard

  • Simplify SAPUI5 Code Extensions with the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard (blog post) https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/simplify-sapui5-code-extensions-with-the-sap-build-extensibility-wizard/ba-p/14087839

SAP Build New Pricing and Packaging

  • Introducing the New Pricing and Packaging for SAP Build: A Unified Experience for all Developers (blog post) https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/introducing-the-new-pricing-and-packaging-for-sap-build-a-unified/ba-p/14086382

 

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 ABAP Challenge for May

1:00 UI5ers live

1:48 New adapters in SAP Integration Suite

2:38 SAP Build Extensibility Wizard

3:46 SAP Build New Pricing and Packaging 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for May the 1st, 2025.

[Mamikee] We have an exciting new developer challenge for the month of May. And yes, it's all about ABAP. This time, we're putting the spotlight on extensibility. That means we'll be getting into areas like adjusting software behavior beyond standard configuration, extending the data model, and exposing additional data, and much more. Now, the ABAP room will be unlocked starting May 5th and will remain open until the end of the year. But remember, the challenge closes on May 31st, so make sure to escape the room before that to earn your badge. Now, just like previous challenges, participating is really easy. All you need to do is visit the SAP Community Escape House, enter your numeric SAP Community user ID, and then you select the ABAP Limit Room. To learn more about the challenge and many more others, click the link in the description below. And remember, have fun!

[Nico] Hi everyone and welcome to the sap developer news we would like to point your attention to the upcoming ui5ers live webinar happening on May 8th and it will be about AI-powered ui5 applications built with LM studio and the speaker of the webinar will be Peter Music the Chief development architect of ui5 and I definitely think you shouldn't miss this one it's open it's free and anybody can join. And in case you're interested in the previous 44 episodes of UI5ers Live, you can watch the recordings of those on the UI5 YouTube channel. I'll put the link to that in the description of this video as well. All right, hope to talk to you soon. Bye.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. In the integration space, this week is all about new adapters available in SAP Integration Suite. There's a new adapter for Anaplan and HubSpot. Interacting with databases there's a new adapter for Azure Cosmos DB. To power your event-driven architectures, the following new adapters are available. Amazon EventBridge, Google PubSub, IBM MQ REST adapter, and Salesforce PubSub adapter. Related to enterprise architecture management, there's a new LeanIX adapter. And last but centrally, not least, the RFC adapter is coming for the Edge Integrational Cell Runtime. If you want to learn more about each adapter, check out the links, including the description. Ciao!

[DJ] There are a lot of different approaches for extending SAP Fiori apps, powered by SAPUI5, of course. There are adaptation projects for key users and for developers, and there's SAPUI5 flexibility. There's a helpful blog post by Oliver Gref that covers these approaches and also mentions the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard that you should definitely check out. The wizard was introduced in the context of SAP S4 HANA Cloud and consolidates multiple extension options. The most recent update added the ability to create sap ui5 adaptation projects making it the go-to facility in sap build for extending sap business suite check out Oliver's blog post it's got lots of really helpful links to the right parts of the SAP help portal on all of this link in the description

[Thomas] Hey, I'm here in the Newtown Square offices with Josh Bentley, and we're working on a workshop around our organization, but I want to talk to you today about SAP Build. Just Announce is new pricing and bundling options for SAP Build. So in the past, if you wanted to use multiple of the capabilities that are part of SAP Build, You had to license and enable them separately, and there's quite a few of those capabilities. Maybe you want to use build code and build process automation and build apps, and that was a lot of work. The powers that be at SAP heard your feedback around that, and they want to make it easier for you to use all the capabilities of SAP Build. Therefore, there's new bundling options where you could purchase SAP Build and a single license that will give you all the capabilities under SAP Build, including the ABAP cloud capabilities. So to read more about all the options, how they're priced, how they're sized, check out Thomas Balmering's blog post. The link to that will be in the description below.

entry.title: System Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: How to find Finance inconsistencies?
entry.content:
When transitioning to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP On-Premise, it is crucial to recognize potential inconsistencies in Finance within the Customizing and the Transactional Data in your Production SAP ERP system.  Recognizing these inconsistencies will prepare you to proactively address and correct issues that arise during the conversion process.

How to detect inconsistencies in the Customizing?

Run the Simplification Item report <SE38> SDF/RC_START_CHECK. The report will display all Simplification Items (SI) relevant to your system.  Any inconsistencies identified in the report need to be addressed and resolved before the Software Update Manager (SUM) is executed.

Tip!  The initial task for the SUM is to run the SI report. If any errors occur while the SUM is in execution, the process will be aborted and will be unable to proceed until the errors are addressed and resolved.

How to detect inconsistencies in the Transactional Data and Totals?

 SAP recommends running specific reports in your Production SAP ERP system to identify any data discrepancies that need to be addressed. These reports will compare data between different table structures within the submodules to ensure accuracy.

Tip! The FIN_XX_CORR_RECONCILE reports examine transactional data specifically designed for the SAP S/4HANA conversion. These programs initiate checks like those performed in SAP S/4HANA by the Finance Data Migration process. For more details about these programs, please refer to my blog post 

System Conversion_ Finance Consistency checks of Transactional Data 

Finance Consistency Checks: FIN_CORR_MONITOR

Material Ledger Consistency Checks: FIN_ML_CORR_RECONCILE

Fixed Assets Consistency Checks: FIN_AA_CORR_RECON & FIN_AA_CORR_DISPLAY

It is important to highlight that the consistency check reports executed in your Production SAP ERP system may not identify all inconsistencies, which are attributed to limitations in the database and table structures. Furthermore, it is essential to acknowledge that the consistency checks available in the SAP ERP system will not be entirely comparable to those executed in the S/4HANA system.

Please be aware that new inconsistencies may arise after converting to SAP S/4HANA and during the Finance Data Migration process (Transaction FINS_MIG_STATUS). It is important to understand that these new inconsistencies could not be identified before reaching this stage of the conversion process, because the ECC system had not yet transitioned to SAP S/4HANA and adopted the new data model in Finance.

In my next blog post, I will explain the proposed SAP Sandbox conversion approach, emphasize the significance of following the outlined steps during your initial Sandbox iteration, and discuss the implications that the results provide for the overall Conversion System project.

For more details about this blog's activities, please check

 SAP Activate for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition  System Conversion> Reconciliation of Financial Data

SAP Activate for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - System Conversion >Functional Pre Conversion Activities

SAP Activate for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - System Conversion> Functional Pre-Conversion Activities: Finance Specific

Conclusion

In summary, when transitioning to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP On-Premise, it is essential to execute a review of Customizing and Transactional Data on your existing SAP ERP system. Identifying inconsistencies early in the process will prepare you to proactively address and correct issues that may arise during the conversion process.

Please “like” if you found this blog post interesting!  

Please add in the comment section any additional topics you think are valuable to have included in this and future blog posts.

– Brought to you by the S/4HANA RIG

entry.title: How to move direct transfer Migration Projects (Fiori app Migration Cockpit) to other systems?

We're using direct transfer Migration Objects in our Migration Cockpit and would like to transport them not only between clients, but also between systems/to higher environments. For instance: from Development to Quality.

We're using an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, 2023, FPS 01.

Thanks!

Best regards,
Data migration team


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to move direct transfer Migration Projects (Fiori...
entry.content:
Dear team,

Indeed, using the transport functionality is the correct way to move/transport Migration Objects/Projects from Development to Quality/Test. You can find more information about this in the link below:

  • Transporting Migration Objects to the Test System | SAP Help Portal

We also have an SAP Note to present the modifiability and transport concepts applied to the direct transfer migration method. It redirects to the same official documentation you linked in your question:

  • 3043614 - Modifiable Systems and Transport Concept, SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit - Migrate Data Directly From SAP

I hope this helps with your query!

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Provisioning between MRP storage areas
entry.content: Thanks Caetano, question: after reviewing SAP APO, will this advanced planning allow me to provision MRP areas from different parts without needing the BADI?
